title: carex pediformis subsp. rhizina
intro: Carex pediformis subsp. rhizina, also known as Rhizine sedge, is a subspecies of the Carex pediformis plant, a type of sedge commonly found in mountainous regions. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits associated with Rhizine sedge include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to aid in the reduction of swelling and pain. In herbal preparations, Carex pediformis subsp. rhizina is often used as a decoction, tea, or infusion to harness its medicinal benefits.
